context: Plain film: √  hepatosplenomegaly √  enlarged azygos vein √  paraspinal varices UGI: √  esophageal varices √  thickening of bowel wall US: √  echogenic material within vessel lumen (67%) / thrombus may be isoechoic √  increase in portal vein diameter (57%): √  enlargement of thrombosed segment > 15 mm (38%) √  portosystemic collateral circulation (48%): biliary and gastric tributaries of portal vein (most common) √  thickening of lesser omentum Doppler-US: √  NO flow on postprandial color Doppler scans: ◊  Malignant thrombus tends to distend vein + exhibit pulsatile flow, a bland thrombus does
